## 1. Überblick über die Lagerung und Behandlung von Abfallströmen
Der Bericht listet detailliert verschiedene Anlagen und Einrichtungen zur Behandlung radioaktiver Abfälle auf, die in unterschiedliche Kategorien unterteilt sind [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

heading_path: 1. Überblick über die Lagerung und Behandlung von Abfallströmen
wiki:summary-jc-8-bericht-deutschland-at.text:
### 1.1. Anlagen zur Konditionierung radioaktiver Abfälle
Es werden Beispiele für stationäre Einrichtungen zur Konditionierung radioaktiver Abfälle für Eigenbedarf und Dritte genannt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md]. Zu diesen gehören:
*   **PETRA Trocknung:** Trocknung von Abfällen in 200-l-Fässern, 280-l-Fässern oder 400-l-Fässern, unter Verwendung einer Hochdruck-Hydraulikpresse FAKIR [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Kompaktierung:** Anlagen zur Kompaktierung von 200-l-Fässern und von Knautschtrommeln mit einem Pressdruck von $\ge 30 \text{ MPa}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Verfestigung und Dekontamination:** Verfahren wie die Zementierung von Abwässern mit Fixierungsmitteln oder die Dekontaminierung von Anlagenteilen mittels Sandstrahlen werden beschrieben [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
Mobile Einrichtungen umfassen unter anderem:
*   **Hochdruck-Hydraulikpresse FAKIR:** Zur Verarbeitung von Abfällen mittels Blechkartuschen zu Presslingen [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Trocknungsanlagen:** Verschiedene Typen (FAVO-RIT, PETRA, KETRA) für flüssige und feste Abfälle [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

heading_path: 1. Überblick über die Lagerung und Behandlung von Abfallströmen
wiki:summary-jc-8-bericht-deutschland-at.text:
### 1.2. Zwischenlager und Endlager
Der Bericht listet zahlreiche Zwischenlager (Zwischenlager) und Endlager (Endlager) auf, die für die sichere Lagerung von radioaktiven Abfällen konzipiert sind [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
**Wichtige Zwischenlagerbeispiele:**
*   **Abfall-Zwischenlager Gorleben (AZG):** Lagerung von radioaktiven Abfällen aus KKW, Medizin, Forschung und Gewerbe, mit einer Gesamtaktivität bis $5 \cdot 10^{18} \text{ Bq}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Abfall-Zwischenlager Ahaus (AZA):** Lagerung von radioaktiven Abfällen aus KKW, mit einer Gesamt-Aktivitätsbegrenzung für den Lagerbereich I von $1,0 \cdot 10^{17} \text{ Bq}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Abfall-Zwischenlager Unterweser 1 (AZU 1):** Lagerung von schwach-radioaktiven Abfällen aus den KKW Unterweser und Stade, mit einer Gesamtaktivität bis $1,85 \cdot 10^{15} \text{ Bq}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
**Endlagerbeispiele:**
*   **Endlager Konrad Salzgitter:** Endlagerung radioaktiver Abfälle mit vernachlässigbarer Wärmeentwicklung, mit einer Genehmigung nach $\text{§ 9b AtG}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Endlager für radioaktive Abfälle Morsleben (ERAM):** Endlagerung von schwach- und mittelradioaktiven Abfällen (SMA) mit überwieiegend kurzlebigen Radionukliden [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## 2. Abgebrannte Brennelemente (Spent Fuel)
Der Bericht enthält detaillierte Tabellen, die die Lagerung von abgebrannten Brennelementen an verschiedenen Standorten auflisten, einschließlich:
*   **Standort Grohnde (BZD), Niedersachsen:** Antragstellung am 20. Dezember 1999, Masse $5,5 \cdot 10^{19} \text{ Bq}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Standort Gundremmingen (BZM), Bayern:** Antragstellung am 25. Februar 2000, Masse $2,4 \cdot 10^{20} \text{ Bq}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Standort Isar (BZI), Bayern:** Antragstellung am 23. Februar 2000, Masse $1,5 \cdot 10^{20} \text{ Bq}$ [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## 3. Zusammenfassende rechtliche und technische Aspekte
Die gesamte Darstellung unterstreicht die Notwendigkeit eines streng regulierten, mehrstufigen Managements von radioaktiven Stoffen, das von der *Atomgesetz* (AtG) und der *Strahlenschutzverordnung* (StrlSchV) abgedeckt wird [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md]. Die Kapazitäten und Genehmigungen der genannten Anlagen dienen der Gewährleistung der Sicherheit bei der Behandlung von radioaktiven Abfällen [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## Überblick über das Gemeinsame Übereinkommen
Das Gemeinsame Übereinkommen dient als internationaler Rahmen, um die Sicherheit von radioaktiven Materialien zu gewährleisten, insbesondere im Hinblick auf die Entsorgung von abgebrannten Brennelementen und radioaktiven Abfällen [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## Kernbereiche der Sicherheit
Die Behandlung und Entsorgung radioaktiver Materialien wird in mehreren Schlüsselbereichen adressiert:

### 1. Sicherheit der Behandlung abgebrannter Brennelemente
Der Umgang mit abgebrannten Brennelementen ist ein zentrales Thema des Übereinkommens [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md]. Die Protokolle müssen sicherstellen, dass die Lagerung und spätere Endlagerung dieser Materialien unter strengsten Sicherheitsstandards erfolgt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

### 2. Sicherheit der Behandlung radioaktiver Abfälle
Das Übereinkommen deckt die gesamte Bandbreite radioaktiver Abfälle ab, von denen die Behandlung und Entsorgung sichergestellt werden muss [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md]. Dies umfasst die Notwendigkeit eines umfassenden Managements, das die gesamte Lebensdauer der Abfälle berücksichtigt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## Regulatorische Implikationen
Die Einhaltung des Joint Convention erfordert die Abstimmung nationaler Gesetzgebungen und Verfahren [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md]. Die Berichtserstellung betont die Notwendigkeit, dass nationale Rechtsrahmen die internationalen Best Practices integrieren, um einen geschlossenen und sicheren Kreislauf für radioaktive Materialien zu gewährleisten [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
---

### Policy and Procedures
The management of spent fuel has seen policy shifts in Germany. Previously, the utilization of fissile materials contained in spent fuel was legally mandatory until 1994. The legislation changed in 1994, allowing power plant operators the choice between reprocessing or direct final disposal [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Reprocessing Prohibition:** Since July 1, 2005, the delivery of spent fuel from commercial electricity generation for reprocessing is prohibited according to § 9a Abs. 1 Atomgesetz (AtG) [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md]. Only direct final disposal of spent fuel located in Germany is permissible [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Disposal:** Spent fuel should be disposed of together with high-level waste from reprocessing. The selection of a final disposal site is covered under the Site Selection Act (StandAG) [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Export Control:** Following amendments to the AtG, the export of spent fuel from research reactors is only permissible for serious non-proliferation reasons or for sufficient supply of German research reactors for medical and other peak research purposes. An exception is the transfer for the purpose of producing waste packages suitable for final disposal in Germany [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

wiki:summary-jc-8-bericht-deutschland-at.anchor: Current Storage Status (As of Report Date)
heading_path: Current Storage Status (As of Report Date)
wiki:summary-jc-8-bericht-deutschland-at.text:
### Current Storage Status (As of Report Date)
*   **Decentralized Interim Storage:** Twelve decentralized interim storage sites for spent fuel were approved, built, and operational. These are designed as dry storage facilities [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Gorleben (BZG):** Licensed for spent fuel from LWRs and HAW glass vitrified high-level waste. The license under § 6 AtG expires in 2034, with a new application planned for 2026 [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Ahaus (BZA):** Licensed for spent fuel from various German LWRs, plus storage for transport/storage casks for research/demonstration reactors (THTR 300, etc.) [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Brunsbüttel (KKB):** The license was revoked by the Federal Administrative Court on January 8, 2015. Current legal basis is an administrative order under § 19 AtG until the new license application process is complete [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

### Principles and Responsibility
*   **Polluter Pays Principle:** All steps for radioactive waste management are subject to the Polluter Pays Principle [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **State Responsibility:** The state has legally obligated waste producers to ensure the proper and safe management of radioactive waste generated during the operation and decommissioning of nuclear facilities (e.g., NPPs and research centers) [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Funding:** The federal government assumed the financing obligation for interim and final storage of radioactive waste from the operators in 2017, utilizing a public law fund [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

### Waste Classification
The Site Selection Act (StandAG) mandates finding a final disposal site guaranteeing optimal safety for one million years. The law distinguishes between:
*   **High-Level Waste (HAA):** Characterized by high activity concentrations and high decay heat output. Includes spent fuel, spent fuel concentrate, and decontamination/washing water [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Low- and Intermediate-Level Waste (SMA):** Waste from operation and decommissioning, such as decommissioned plant parts, ion exchange resins, contaminated tools, and contaminated concrete structures [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## Technical Aspects
*   **Waste Conditioning:** Radioactive waste requires conditioning for deep geological disposal. This involves processes to ensure the waste is either directly disposable or can be treated into a disposable form [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Storage Locations:** Interim storage is available at central and decentralized sites, including the Landessammelstellen operated by the states, and specialized interim storage facilities at research centers (e.g., Jülich, Rossendorf, Braunschweig, Geesthacht, Helmholtz-Zentrum Berlin) [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Konrad Site:** The Konrad repository is approved exclusively for radioactive waste with negligible heat development, covering weak and most intermediate-level waste [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## 1. Inventar bestrahlter Brennelemente (Stichtag 31. Dezember 2023)

### 1.1 Leistungsreaktoren (LWR)
*   **Gesamtmenge:** Insgesamt waren 16.711 Mg SM bestrahlte Brennelemente aus deutschen Leichtwasserreaktoren (LWR) mit Leistungen > 50 MW angefallen [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Lagerbecken:** In den Lagerbecken der Kraftwerke befinden sich insgesamt 1.896 Mg SM bestrahlte Brennelemente [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Zwischenlager:** In dezentralen Brennelemente-Zwischenlagern werden 7.463 Mg SM und in zentralen Zwischenlagern in Ahaus und Gorleben 92 Mg SM aufbewahrt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Verbringung:** 6.673 Mg SM wurden zur Wiederaufarbeitung oder zum dauerhaften Verbleib im Ausland abtransportiert und entsorgt, wobei der größte Teil nach La Hague und Sellafield ging [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

### 1.2 Versuchs- und Demonstrationsreaktoren
*   **Gesamtmenge:** Rund 190 Mg SM an bestrahlten Brennelementen von acht Versuchs- und Demonstrationsreaktoren wurden gelagert oder entsorgt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Betreiber:** Die Hauptverarbeitung erfolgte in Karlsruhe (WAK), BNFL und EUROCHEMIC in Belgien [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **AVR-Behälterlager Jülich:** Hier lagern 288.161 AVR-Brennelementkugeln mit 1,9 Mg SM (einschließlich Thorium) in 152 Behältern [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

### 1.3 Aktivitätsinventar
*   **Gesamtaktivität:** Das Gesamtaktivitätsinventar aller gelagerten bestrahlten Brennelemente betrug ca. $2,04 \cdot 10^{20}$ Bq zum 31. Dezember 2023 [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## 2. Anlagen und Einrichtungen zur Behandlung radioaktiver Abfälle

heading_path: 2. Anlagen und Einrichtungen zur Behandlung radioaktiver Abfälle
wiki:summary-jc-8-bericht-deutschland-at.text:
### 2.1 Zwischenlager
Es werden verschiedene Arten von radioaktiven Abfällen behandelt, die zwischengelagert werden müssen:
*   **Zwischenlager Ahaus (AZA):** Neben bestrahlten Brennelementen werden auch schwach- und mittelradioaktive Abfälle (SMA) zwischengelagert. Die Genehmigung für die Zwischenlagerung von Betriebs- und Stilllegungsabfällen ist bis zum Jahr 2057 befristet [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Zwischenlager Nord (ZLN) in Rubenow:** Hier werden neben bestrahlten Brennelementen aus sowjetischen Reaktoren auch bestrahlte und unbestrahlte Brennstäbe aus der KNK II sowie hochradioaktive Glaskokillen aus der WAK gelagert [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Weitere Standorte:** Weitere Zwischenlager umfassen AZU 1 und AZU 2, AZB 1 und AZB 2, AZG, AZO, AZP, AZS, AZW, die EVU-Halle Mitterteich, das Zwischenlager der Orano NCS GmbH in Hanau, das ZLN in Rubenow, das Zwischenlager Rossendorf (ZLR) [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

heading_path: 2. Anlagen und Einrichtungen zur Behandlung radioaktiver Abfälle
wiki:summary-jc-8-bericht-deutschland-at.text:
### 2.2 Endlager Konrad
*   **Standort:** Das Endlager Konrad wird in einem ehemaligen Eisenerzbergwerk in Salzgitter im Land Niedersachsen errichtet [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Kapazität:** Das Einlagerungsvolumen wurde mit maximal 303.000 m$^3$ Abfallgebindevolumen begrenzt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Status:** Die Fertigstellung, ursprünglich für 2027 geplant, wird nun auf das Ende des Jahres 2029 revidiert geschätzt [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

## 3. Konditionierung und Verfahren
*   **Ziel:** Das Ziel der Abfallkonditionierung ist es, radioaktive Abfälle durch Behandlung und/oder Verpackung in eine endlagerfähige Form zu überführen, wobei eine Volumenreduzierung angestrebt wird [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Verfahren:** Es kommen unterschiedliche Verfahren zur Anwendung, je nach Zusammensetzung (organisch, metallisch, mineralisch) und Zustand (fest, flüssig). Beispiele sind Verbrennung, Pyrolyse, Kompaktierung, Schmelzen, Zerkleinerung, Trocknen, Zementieren oder Verglasen [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].
*   **Abklinglagerung:** Neben der Zwischenlagerung wird auch der Weg der Abklinglagerung verfolgt, um eine spätere vereinfachte Verarbeitung und ggf. Freigabe der Stoffe zu ermöglichen [jc_8_bericht_deutschland_atomenergie_bf.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:263b2.text:
Radon-Handbuch_BfS summary-radon-handbuch-bfs

The *Radon-Handbuch_BfS* provides comprehensive, technical guidelines for the assessment, measurement, and remediation of radon concentrations in indoor environments, detailing best practices for radiation protection beyond general regulatory requirements.
The *Radon-Handbuch_BfS* provides comprehensive guidance on the risks associated with Radon and its decay products in indoor environments, detailing measurement techniques, risk assessment, and remediation strategies [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md]. The document emphasizes that the danger from natural radioactivity cannot be perceived by the senses and can only be proven through measurements [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:fe01a.text:
## Key facts
- Radon-Handbuch_BfS
- Overview and Scope
- Radon Chemistry and Radioactivity
- Radioactivity and Decay
- Health Risks

The *Radon-Handbuch_BfS* provides comprehensive information regarding the radioactive noble gas Radon ($\text{Rn}$), its natural occurrence, the associated health risks, and the necessary protective measures under German law [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:a0cff.text:
## Overview and Scope
The handbook explains how Radon, which emanates from the earth, can accumulate in buildings. A long-term stay in indoor environments with elevated Radon concentrations demonstrably increases the risk of lung cancer [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md]. Consequently, the law has incorporated Radon protection into the *Strahlenschutzgesetz* (StrlSchG), which has been fully in force since the end of 2018, alongside the new *Strahlenschutzverordnung* [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:5e83d.text:
## Radon Chemistry and Radioactivity
Radon ($\text{Rn}$) is a radioactive chemical element with the symbol $\text{Rn}$ and atomic number 86. It belongs to the noble gases in the 8th group of the periodic table [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:9bd34.text:
### Radioactivity and Decay
In nature, the radioactive isotopes Radon-219, Radon-220, and Radon-222 occur. Radon-222 ($\text{Rn}$) is particularly significant for human radiation exposure [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Decay Chain:** Radon-222 is formed in the decay series of Uranium-238 via several intermediate steps involving various Radium isotopes [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Decay Products:** The decay of Radon-222 produces several radioactive decay products, including isotopes of Polonium, Bismuth, and Lead (e.g., Polonium-218, Bismuth-214, Lead-214, Polonium-214) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Hazard:** The decay products are radioactive heavy metals that become deposited in the respiratory tract. The energy-rich alpha radiation ($\alpha$-Strahlung) emitted during this process can damage the cells of the lung tissue [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Measurement:** The measure of activity is the Becquerel ($\text{Bq}$) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

## Health Risks

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:75a12.text:
### Lung Cancer Risk
*   **Evidence:** The link between Radon and lung cancer was first observed in the 16th century among young miners in the Erzgebirge, known as "Schneeberger Krankheit" [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Classification:** The WHO International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) classified Radon as definitively carcinogenic to humans in 1988 [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Risk Assessment:** Studies consistently show an increased lung cancer risk from Radon in homes [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   The risk increases proportionally to the long-term Radon concentration in the apartment. Doubling the concentration doubles the risk [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   There is no evidence of a threshold below which Radon would be harmless. Therefore, Radon concentration should be reduced in all apartments if feasible [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   For every $100 \text{ Bq/m}^3$ of long-term Radon concentration, the lung cancer risk increases by approximately 16% [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   Radon is stated to be the second most important risk factor for lung cancer after smoking [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Other Effects:** Radon can also cause radiation exposure to organs other than the lungs, particularly the nose and throat, but the resulting dose is significantly lower than that for the lungs [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

## Occurrence and Accumulation

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:55cc0.text:
### In Buildings
*   **Concentration:** Annual mean values for Radon activity concentration typically range between $5 \text{ Bq/m}^3$ and about $30 \text{ Bq/m}^3$. The average annual mean concentration in residential rooms in Germany is $50 \text{ Bq/m}^3$ [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Accumulation:** Higher concentrations can occur in areas where Radon concentrations in the soil air are high and good underground pathways exist, especially if the structural sealing against the subsoil is inadequate, potentially reaching several hundred $\text{Bq/m}^3$ [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Dose Calculation:** From the average Radon concentration in residential rooms of $50 \text{ Bq/m}^3$, an annual effective dose of $0.85 \text{ mSv}$ results, compared to an annual effective dose of about $0.2 \text{ mSv}$ from the average outdoor Radon concentration [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:e14f7.text:
### Sources of Radon
The sources of Radon in indoor air are listed in Table 4.1 [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md]:
| Radon Source | Typical Range | Maximum |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Subsoil | $10 – 2.000$ | $> 30.000$ |
| Building Material | $< 10 – 70$ | $< 2.000$ |
| Ambient Air | $5 – 30$ | $< 250$ |
| Water | $< 10$ | $< 100$ |

*   **Subsoil:** Radon enters primarily from the subsoil. The available amount depends on the Radon activity concentration in the soil air and the supply from the weathered area of the soil (the so-called loose zone) or along preferred pathways in the rock [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Building Materials:** Radon is continuously generated in mineral building materials as a decay product of Radium. Under normal conditions, the current building materials enclosing the room contribute at most $15 \text{ Bq/m}^3$ to the Radon concentration in the room air. However, certain materials, such as porous concrete made from alunite shale, have been shown to release significantly higher amounts [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Ambient Air:** The concentration in the open air is crucial as it represents a small but non-negligible contribution to the radiation exposure of the population [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Water:** Radon is highly soluble in water and transported by groundwater. While using water (e.g., showering) generally contributes only a few $\text{Bq/m}^3$ to the annual average, under specific geological conditions (e.g., granite areas or uranium ore deposits), individual wells could contribute up to $10 \text{ Bq/m}^3$ to the Radon concentration in residential rooms [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

## Protection Measures

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:97ac4.text:
### Legal Requirements
*   **General Principle:** The protection against Radon is regulated by the *Strahlenschutzgesetz* and the *Strahlenschutzverordnung* [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Reference Value:** A reference value of $300 \text{ Becquerel per cubic meter}$ is set in the law for high Radon concentrations in indoor rooms. If this value is exceeded, measures must be taken to reduce the concentration [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Mandatory Action:**
    *   **New Builds:** For new constructions in designated Radon prevention areas, builders have a fundamental duty to prevent Radon from entering the building through structural measures.
    *   **Workplaces:** If a workplace is located in a Radon prevention area, the responsible parties must measure the Radon concentration in basements and ground floors. If the concentration exceeds $300 \text{ Bq/m}^3$, immediate reduction measures must be implemented [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Residential Buildings:** In existing residential buildings, Radon protection is voluntary; the law does not impose an obligation to act [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:49af3.text:
### Technical Measures
*   **Ventilation:** Ventilation with widely open windows allows most Radon to escape into the outside air. The Radon concentration in an indoor room depends on the air exchange rate ($K$) and the exhalation surface area ($A$) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Sealing:** Measures include the targeted influence of the air pressure difference between the interior and the subsoil, restricting cracking in walls and floors in contact with the earth, using diffusion-inhibiting concrete types, or extracting Radon at joints or under seals [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
# Radon-Handbuch_BfS: Maßnahmen zum Radonschutz
Dieses Dokument fasst technische und bautechnische Maßnahmen zur Prävention und Sanierung von erhöhter Radonkonzentration in Gebäuden zusammen. Der Schutz vor Radon ist ein komplexes Feld, das sowohl bauliche Abdichtungen als auch lüftungstechnische Konzepte umfasst [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:42e7b.text:
## Konzeptionelle Maßnahmen und Vorausschauende Planung
Die Planung von Neubau- und Sanierungsvorhaben muss die potenzielle Radonexposition berücksichtigen. Es wird betont, dass jede Strategie, die Aufenthaltsräume vom Erdreich „abkoppelt“, hilfreich ist [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Wichtige Planungsaspekte:**
*   **Veränderungen vermeiden:** Der nachträgliche Um- oder Ausbau von Kellerräumen in Radonvorsorgebieten kann die Radonsituation verändern [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Prävention beim Neubau:** Es muss garantiert werden, dass auch Kellerräume, die nur zu Lagerzwecken dienen, keine erhöhte Radonkonzentration aufweisen, um einen späteren Ausbau als Aufenthaltsräume zu ermöglichen, und es sollte keine offenen Verbindungen zwischen Kellerräumen und Wohnbereichen geben [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Raumnutzung:** Räume mit erhöhter Radonkonzentration sollten für Nutzungen mit geringerer Aufenthaltsdauer konzipiert werden (z. B. Abstell- und Lagerraum) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Prävention beim Neubau:** Es muss garantiert werden, dass auch Kellerräume, die nur zu Lagerzwecken dienen, keine erhöhte Radonkonzentration aufweisen, um einen späteren Ausbau als Aufenthaltsräume zu ermöglichen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Trennung:** Offene Verbindungen zwischen Kellerräumen und Wohnbereichen, wie offene Treppenhäuser bis ins Kellergeschoss, begünstigen die Radonausbreitung. Kellerbereiche sollten daher in Radonvorsorgebieten mindestens durch eine dichte Tür von der Wohnung abgetrennt sein. Ein separater Kellerzugang von außen wird als noch besser erachtet [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:ee2a2.text:
## Wärmeschutz, Heizung und Lüftung
Das Heizungs-/Lüftungssystem bestimmt maßgeblich den Austausch zwischen Innen- und Außenluft sowie die Radonkonzentration. Bei energetischen Sanierungen muss die potenzielle Reduktion des Luftwechsels berücksichtigt werden, da dies zur Zunahme von Innenraumschadstoffen, einschließlich Radon, führen kann [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Leitungsführungen:**
*   Jede Durchdringung der Bauteile zum Erdreich hin ist eine potenzielle Eintrittsstelle für Radon [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Wasser- und Gasleitungen sollten, wenn möglich, durch die Wände und nicht durch den Boden zugeführt werden [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Erdsonden für Wärmepumpen bilden Radon-Sammelschächte erster Güte und sollten nicht unter, sondern deutlich neben dem Gebäude angeordnet werden [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:5e56b.text:
## Bautechnische Maßnahmen – Abdichtung gegen Radonzufuhr
Der Schutz erfordert eine geschlossene „Gebäudeumhüllung“ gegen das Erdreich, vergleichbar mit dem Schutz vor Bodenfeuchte [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:7ab44.text:
### Diffusions- und Konvektionstransport
Radon kann auf zwei Arten eindringen:
1.  **Konvektiv:** Als Bestandteil von Bodenluft, die durch Leckstellen oder Öffnungen in erdberührenden Bauteilen aufgrund von Unterdruck eindringt [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
2.  **Diffusiv:** Durch Boden und Wände aufgrund eines Konzentrationsgefälles [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
Die Gasdurchlässigkeit ist abhängig von der Struktur des Materials. Für die Beurteilung der Dichtigkeit wird ein Material als radondicht bezeichnet, wenn die Materialdicke mindestens das Dreifache der Diffusionslänge beträgt [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Baumaterialien:**
*   Geeignete Materialien für die Abdichtung umfassen: Anstriche, Beschichtungen, PEHD (1 mm) Dichtungsbahnen, PVC legiert (1 mm), Polymerbitumen (5 mm), Kunstharzlack (0,2 mm), Epoxydharz (3 mm), Normalbeton (100 mm), Kalksandstein (150 mm), Gips (100 mm), Mauerziegel (150 mm) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Die Dichtigkeit ist gefährdet, wenn Dichtungsbahnen Risse aufweisen oder Überlappungen nicht gasdicht verklebt/verschweißt wurden [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
Die Gasdurchlässigkeit ist abhängig von der Struktur des Materials. Edelgase wie Radon diffundieren besonders gut durch poröse Stoffe. Für die Beurteilung der Dichtigkeit wird ein Material als radondicht bezeichnet, wenn die Materialdicke mindestens das Dreifache der Diffusionslänge beträgt [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:6fe53.text:
### Leckstellen und Abdichtungsvarianten
Für erhöhte Radonkonzentrationen ist die Unterbindung des konvektiven Transports durch Undichtigkeiten entscheidend. Quellen für Undichtigkeiten sind unter anderem:
*   Risse und Fugen in Böden und Wänden [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Durchführungen von Kabeln, Leitungen und Leerrohren [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Abwasserrohre, Bodenschächte, Kontrollöffnungen, Kamine [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
Die Abdichtung sollte immer eine Mehrfachstrategie umfassen und umfasst:
*   Absperren des Erdreichs mit Dichtungsbahnen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Dichtungsbahnen und Anstrichdichtungen in und an Bauteilen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Dichten von Fugen, Rissen, Löchern und Durchbrüchen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   Dichten von Öffnungen (Türen, Fenstern, Klappen, Deckel etc.) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:98171.text:
### Lüftungstechnische Maßnahmen
Die Druckverhältnisse sind entscheidend. Ein Unterdruckgefälle vom Erdreich zum Gebäudeinneren treibt die Radoninfiltration an [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Strategien zur Druckkontrolle:**
1.  **Unterdruck erzeugende Faktoren eliminieren:** Dazu gehören beispielsweise technische Anlagen wie Abluftventilatoren oder Kamine, wenn keine Nachströmöffnungen vorhanden sind [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
2.  **Künstlichen Überdruck erzeugen:** Dies ist ein Ziel, um das Eindringen von Radon zu verhindern [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
3.  **Abführen radonhaltiger Luft:** Dies kann passiv oder aktiv (ventilatorbetrieben) erfolgen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Vorsicht bei aktiven Systemen:** Aktive Lüftungssysteme benötigen eine Stromversorgung und müssen immer als zweite Stufe betrachtet werden, falls passive Systeme keinen Erfolg zeigen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Vorsicht bei aktiven Systemen:** Aktive Lüftungssysteme benötigen eine Stromversorgung und müssen immer als zweite Stufe betrachtet werden, falls passive Systeme versagen [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:a570d.text:
## Radon Exposure Assessment
The assessment of radon exposure is critical, as the resulting health risk is derived from long-term radon exposure [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:d7f0a.text:
### Measurement Principles
The determination of radon levels requires measurements in the most important living areas, ideally capturing annual mean values [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md]. The radon concentration in indoor rooms is dependent not only on the radon sources and diffusion paths but also significantly on weather conditions, the type of room usage, room climate control, and user behavior, leading to strong temporal fluctuations [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Reference Values:** The BfS considers measures for reducing radon levels advisable from a value of 100 Bq/m³ in the annual mean in frequently used indoor rooms. The evaluation of measurement results checks whether the reference value of 300 Bq/m³ is exceeded [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Measurement Types:**
    *   **Long-Term Measurement:** Measurements should ideally cover a period of one year [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   **Short-Term Measurement:** Short-term measurements can provide initial clues regarding sources and diffusion paths [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   **Gas Tracing:** Using tracer gases and passive PFT (Polyfluorcarbon-Tracergas)-methods allows for a quantitative description of exchange processes between indoor and outdoor air, and between different rooms, providing detailed insights into diffusion paths and radon transport streams [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:a049f.text:
### Measurement Techniques
Several specialized techniques are recommended for accurate measurement:
*   **Radon Sniffing:** This technique involves using air-sucking probes at prominent cracks and discontinuities in materials to provide clues about whether these spots are relevant for radon diffusion [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Gamma Site Dose Rate (ODL) Measurement:** ODL measurements aim to identify materials with elevated Radium-226 concentrations. These measurements should be performed:
    *   In the immediate vicinity of the building to search for accumulations of material with elevated Radium-226 concentration, as well as for radiating passages and layers with elevated Radium concentration or other geogen-induced Radium anomalies (Note: The detection depth is only approx. 30 cm) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   Inside the building to locate construction and filling materials with elevated Radium-226 concentration [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   The primary measurement point should be the center of the room at a height of 1 m above the floor. Secondary points should represent the ODL in 10 cm from the floor, ceiling, and wall surfaces [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:a880d.text:
## Radon Sources and Pathways
High radon concentrations can result from multiple sources and pathways:
*   **Sources:** Primarily a geogenically elevated radon potential in the building subsoil. Other sources include surface-level mine structures that approach or run near the buildings, and building foundations/fillings using mine material [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Entry Pathways:** Key entry pathways for soil radon include:
    *   Insufficiently sealed ground-contact building areas (e.g., floors and walls of basements).
    *   Leaks in walls and floors.
    *   Material discontinuity points, such as next to utility lines.
    *   Building-connected sewage systems [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Diffusion Pathways:** Key diffusion pathways within buildings include:
    *   Free structural connections within the buildings (stair areas, doors, utility shafts, conduit runs).
    *   Non-radon-sealed floor/ceiling structures.
    *   Leaky masonry, material discontinuity, and instability points [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:fcbbc.text:
## Remediation and Mitigation Strategies
Remediation efforts must be based on a comprehensive understanding of the sources, entry paths, and diffusion paths [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:8a82b.text:
### General Principles
1.  **Site Assessment:** The analysis of site-specific factors (permeability of the subsoil, influence of mining, groundwater level) is as important as the radon measurements themselves [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
2.  **Design Solutions (Neubau/Umbau):** Architectural solutions, such as appropriate structuring of buildings and arranging rooms with short occupancy times in basement areas, can influence the radon problem [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
3.  **Technical Measures:** Implementing radon-sealed construction structures and elements in new builds, or sealing measures in renovations (e.g., sealing utility lines from the subsoil or sealing connecting doors against radon-affected rooms), can prevent radon entry and diffusion. These measures must also be designed to avoid harmful condensation [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
4.  **Ventilation:** Creating defined pressure differences (negative or positive pressure) can reduce or eliminate radon entry. When operating under positive pressure, care must be taken to prevent building-damaging condensation, especially at low outdoor temperatures [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
5.  **Quality Control:** Success depends on continuous monitoring. Measurements must be conducted to verify the effectiveness of implemented measures, such as checking the radon-tightness of newly installed structural elements or the sealing effectiveness of injection points [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:de339.text:
### Measurement Standards
For high-quality results, specific standards must be adhered to:
*   **Passive Devices:** For passive radon measuring devices, adherence to DIN 11665-4 is recommended.
*   **Electronic Devices:** For electronic radon measuring devices, DIN EN 61577-2 is recommended [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
# Radon-Handbuch_BfS (Teil 4/5)
This document provides comprehensive technical guidelines for the prevention and remediation of radon gas in buildings, covering both new constructions (Neubau) and existing buildings (Bestand). It details various sealing, ventilation, and engineering measures, emphasizing the synergy between different protective strategies.

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:4c775.text:
## General Principles and Synergies
The effectiveness of radon protection relies on a systematic approach, noting that successful mitigation often requires combining multiple measures [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Synergies:** The document highlights that measures for moisture protection (Feuchte- und Grundwasserschutz) align completely with radon protection, as both require the sealing of leaks. Similarly, soundproofing elements (Schallschutz) can contribute to radon tightness.
*   **Conflict Potential:** A potential conflict arises with thermal protection (Wärmeschutz): If the building envelope is insulated and air exchange is reduced, or if new heating systems are installed without sealing potential radon pathways from the ground, the radon concentration inside the house could be higher than previously measured [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Planning Approach:** Radon prevention should be integrated into the planning phase, distinguishing between "Standard-Gebäudeschutz" and "besondere Radonprävention" based on the assessed radon load of the site [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:c2465.text:
## Radon Mitigation Measures (Radonsanierung)
The guidelines differentiate between simple and comprehensive remediation strategies:

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:639c6.text:
### 1. Sealing Measures (Abdichtung)
Sealing is crucial for both primary and secondary containment perimeters:
*   **Primary Sealing Perimeter:** Involves sealing penetrations, joints, and cracks in structural elements against earth [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Secondary Sealing Perimeter:** Involves sealing walls, ceilings, and doors between living and basement areas [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Best Practice:** The document stresses the importance of airtightness and suggests that the planning should aim for minimal penetrations of the building envelope through utility lines or shafts [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:8118f.text:
### 2. Ventilation and Ventilation Systems (Lüftung)
Ventilation is a key active measure, requiring careful design to avoid negative side effects:
*   **Pressure Management:** The goal is often to create a slight positive pressure (Überdruck) in the living areas relative to the sub-ground areas, or to manage negative pressure (Unterdruck) in the sub-ground areas [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **System Types:**
    *   **Mechanical Ventilation with Heat Recovery (Wärmerückgewinnung):** This is recommended as it allows for high air exchange rates without excessive energy loss, even in winter [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   **Under-pressure Creation:** Can be achieved via dedicated ventilation systems or by creating a vacuum in the sub-ground area [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Drainage/Ventilation of Sub-ground Areas:** Methods include:
    *   **Drainage Pipes (Drainagerohre):** Collecting radon-laden soil air and venting it outside. Effectiveness depends on achieving a full-area under-pressure [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   **Central Collection Shafts (Sammelschacht):** Collecting soil air below the floor structure, effective if a continuous void exists or if the subsoil has high permeability (e.g., gravel) [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
    *   **External Radon Wells (Radonbrunnen):** Establishing wells outside the building can lead to cost-effective remediation, as they can create a large under-pressure area in the surrounding soil [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Technical Requirements for Fans (Ventilatoren):** Fans must have high lifespan (e.g., minimum 80,000 hours), adjustable speed, and a visible operating indicator. Efficiency is critical, as operational costs can exceed €500 over 10 years [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:1a027.text:
### 3. Specific Building Scenarios
*   **Basement/Sub-ground Ventilation:** If radon enters primarily from the sub-ground, increased air exchange in the basement (e.g., cross-ventilation) can dilute the concentration. However, this must be balanced against the risk of severe cooling in winter [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Modernization:** When renovating, it is recommended to proceed stepwise, distinguishing between "simple" and "comprehensive" remediation, as comprehensive measures are significantly more complex than those for new builds [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:49776.text:
## General Principles and Risk Communication
The handbook stresses the need for public education, advising citizens to be informed by factual information based on science and technology, rather than succumbing to fear regarding potential "radiation exposure" [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md]. The goal is to show actionable protective measures for inhabitants, building owners, and professionals [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:fe8c2.text:
## Remediation Potential of Remediation Measures
The potential for reducing Radon concentrations varies significantly depending on the implemented measure [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
| Remediation Method | Achievable Reductions of Radon Concentration | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Simple sealing measures | 0 to 25 % | Applicable in areas up to 1,000 Bq/m³ |
| Negative pressure maintenance in basements, basement ventilation using small fan | 50 to 80 % | |
| Elaborate insulation layers in or on floors without involving walls | 30 to 80 % | |
| Elaborate insulation layers in or on floors involving walls | 50 to 90 % | Walls sealed continuously with foil |
| Negative pressure maintenance within floor constructions involving walls | 10 to 90 % | |
| *Result strongly depends on the type of subsequent walls* | | |
| Negative pressure maintenance under the building (drainage system point suction) | 10 to 95 % | Depending on soil permeability and location of drainage pipes |
| Elaborate insulation in newly inserted floors, negative pressure maintenance under the building | up to 99 % | With a continuous sealing layer through the entire building, except for unavoidable weak points |

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:8f3cc.anchor: Inspection and Evidence Preservation Checklist (Checkliste Beweissicherung/Hausbegehung)
heading_path: Inspection and Evidence Preservation Checklist (Checkliste Beweissicherung/Hausbegehung)
wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:8f3cc.text:
## Inspection and Evidence Preservation Checklist (Checkliste Beweissicherung/Hausbegehung)
The BfS recommends analyzing buildings with Radon concentrations exceeding 100 Bq/m³ in living areas to determine if a successful remediation is possible [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md]. The inspection must focus on identifying potential entry and dispersion paths of radon gas [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
**Key Inspection Areas:**
*   **Boundaries (Abgrenzungen):** Determining which structures and rooms border the ground. All known areas must be mapped.
*   **Basements (Keller):** Moisture and odor suggest insufficient air exchange and/or damp spots. Radon concentration measurement in affected rooms is necessary [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Living Areas (Aufenthaltsräume):** Odor and moisture are indicators. In ground-floor rooms directly above ground, the same considerations as basements apply.
*   **Floor in the lowest story (Boden im untersten Geschoss):** Assessing the construction material (natural soil, floorboards, slabs, stones, continuous concrete slab). Checking for cracks or holes. The state of the joint structure must be examined, as flat, linear, or point weak spots are potential leak zones [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Basement walls against the ground (Kellerwände gegen das Erdreich):** Assessing materials (e.g., hollow walls, rubble stone, reinforced concrete). Checking for cracks, holes, and the condition of joints to the floor and ceiling.
*   **Basement walls against living areas (Kellerwände gegen Aufenthaltsräume):** Assessing materials and joint conditions. The upper closure of intermediate walls is often leaky. Multi-shelled walls can create ideal dispersion paths [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Basement windows, ventilation openings (Kellerfenster, Lüftungsöffnungen):** Documenting material and condition.
*   **Pipes/Lines from/to outside (Leitungen von/nach außen):** Checking if utility lines (electrical mains, TV cables, gas mains, water/sewage pipes, oil lines, ventilation pipes, heat pump ground source lines, etc.) are sealed against radon and the masonry/floor.

wiki:summary-radon-handbuch-bfs.md:ffa9d.text:
## Technical Appendices (ANHANG)
The appendix provides detailed inspection points for various building systems, including:
*   **Pipes and lines inside the building:** Electrical lines, sewage pipes, hot/cold water pipes, phone cables, gas pipes, TV cables, chimneys, and heating pipes [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Heating:** Checking combustion air supply, chimney flaps, and the closure status of the chimney explosion flap.
*   **Crawlspaces (Kriechkeller):** Assessing structural and building condition and ventilation status.
*   **Basement Ceiling/Floor:** Assessing construction, visible cracks, and joint conditions.
*   **Basement Doors:** Special attention must be paid to the sealing capability of the doors and their thresholds [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].
*   **Overall Pressure Situation:** Analyzing the pressure situation in the building, which varies by day and year, and the ventilation setup [Radon-Handbuch_BfS.md].

## Key facts

This document summarizes excerpts from the *Gesetz zum Schutz vor der schädlichen Wirkung ionisierender Strahlung* (Strahlenschutzgesetz - StrlSchG) from Teil 7/13, detailing various regulatory powers, reporting obligations, and emergency planning procedures related to ionizing radiation.

The *Strahlenschutzgesetz* (StrlSchG) is the German law concerning the protection against the harmful effects of ionizing radiation. It is defined as the "Gesetz zum Schutz vor der schädlichen Wirkung ionisierender Strahlung (Strahlenschutzgesetz - StrlSchG) vom 27.06.2017" [StrlSchG.md].

heading_path: § 89 Verordnungsermächtigungen zu der Sicherheit von Strahlungsquellen
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:ad11fc3f0dfcffb.text:
### § 89 Verordnungsermächtigungen zu der Sicherheit von Strahlungsquellen
The Federal Government is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to determine measures for the protection of people against the harmful effects of ionizing radiation and for the control and securing of radioactive materials. These measures include provisions regarding:
1.  Maintaining a register on the generation, acquisition, possession, location, disposal, and other whereabouts of radioactive materials, and regarding measurements of dose and dose rates, including the manner of reporting and document retention.
2.  Requirements for the securing and storage of radioactive materials.
3.  Requirements for the maintenance and inspection of equipment, devices, and other apparatus, and who must perform this maintenance and inspection.
4.  Requirements for leak testing of enclosed radioactive materials and who must perform the leak testing.
5.  Which radiation measuring devices must be used and what requirements apply to them.
6.  Which areas, rooms, devices, apparatus, containers, enclosures, facilities for the generation of ionizing radiation, and which type-approved devices must be marked, how and under what conditions the marking must occur, and in which cases markings must be removed.
7.  Requirements for the disposal of radioactive materials.
8.  Requirements for the return of high-radioactive sources.
9.  In which cases X-ray rooms or irradiation rooms must be used for activities involving radiation sources, and what requirements apply to these rooms.
10. Which persons are permitted to apply radiation or technically perform the application during activities involving radiation sources, how and when persons must be instructed during such activities, and what documents must be available when performing these activities, including the requirement to keep records of the instructions and present them to the authority upon request.
11. Further preventive and monitoring measures for the control of radioactive materials for the protection of individuals and the general public, and what such measures must be.
12. Further obligations regarding recording, retention, notification, submission, and deposit in connection with the obligations under numbers 1 to 11.
The ordinance can also stipulate provisions of the ordinance for whose compliance the radiation protection officer must ensure. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 90 Verordnungsermächtigung für Pflichten, Aufgaben und Befugnisse bei Vorkommnissen; Aufzeichnungs-, Übermittlungs- und Aufbewahrungspflichten
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:e667b90f887f820.text:
### § 90 Verordnungsermächtigung für Pflichten, Aufgaben und Befugnisse bei Vorkommnissen; Aufzeichnungs-, Übermittlungs- und Aufbewahrungspflichten
The Federal Government is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to stipulate the duties of the radiation protection officer, as well as the tasks and powers of the authorities, concerning incidents in planned exposure situations. Specifically, the ordinance may stipulate:
1.  What measures the radiation protection officer must initiate to keep exposures in such an incident as low as possible.
2.  What measures the radiation protection officer must take to prevent such incidents in the future.
3.  How the radiation protection officer must record and investigate an incident, and for what period they must retain related records.
4.  How the radiation protection officer must report an incident to the supervisory authority, and must report information and findings on the causes and effects of the incident, as well as measures for remediation or limitation of the effects, and measures for preventing incidents.
5.  How the supervisory authority records, checks, and evaluates reports according to number 4.
6.  How the Federal Office for Radiation Protection maintains a central body for the recording, processing, and evaluation of information and findings on incidents related to the application of radioactive materials or ionizing radiation.
7.  How the supervisory authority and the central body transmit information and findings on an incident related to the application of radioactive materials or ionizing radiation to humans, and their respective evaluations.
8.  Under what conditions and in what manner the supervisory authority and the central body publish information and findings on incidents.
(2) The radiation protection officer must ensure that in the event of an incident subject to Paragraph 1 of the ordinance, the name, first names, date and place of birth, gender, and address, as well as data on the exposure of the exposed person due to the incident and the health consequences of the exposure are immediately recorded. If the radiation protection officer must report the incident according to the ordinance, and measures are required to protect the exposed person, they must immediately transmit the data to the competent authority. The data must be secured against unauthorized access by technical and organizational measures. In other cases than in sentence 2, the data must be transmitted to the competent authority upon request. The data must be retained for 30 years and must be deleted immediately after the expiration of this period. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 91 Verordnungsermächtigung für Informationspflichten des Herstellers oder Lieferanten von Geräten
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:2d294412c832004.text:
### § 91 Verordnungsermächtigung für Informationspflichten des Herstellers oder Lieferanten von Geräten
The Federal Government is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to stipulate that the manufacturer or supplier of facilities for the generation of ionizing radiation, X-ray equipment, scatter radiation, irradiation devices, and other equipment, devices, and apparatus used in connection with activities, must provide the radiation protection officer with information about these devices. Specifically, the ordinance may stipulate:
1.  When the manufacturer or supplier must provide information to the radiation protection officer for which of the mentioned devices.
2.  What data and documents must be provided.
3.  For what purposes the documents must be suitable and what requirements they must meet.
4.  That the information must also be available to the person intending to become the radiation protection officer. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:019f99bacc06ae2.text:
### § 92 Notfallschutzgrundsätze
(1) The provisions of the following paragraphs (Emergency Protection Principles) must be considered as requirements when assessing dangers that may arise in emergencies due to ionizing radiation in the following cases:
1. When enacting, reviewing, and amending emergency plans and ordinances under this chapter and under § 117.
2. During the emergency response by the competent authorities and the authorities and organizations assisting in the emergency response based on this law, the ordinances mentioned in number 1, and federal and state legal provisions for the defense against dangers for human health, the environment, or public safety, insofar as they are applicable to radiological dangers, and immediately applicable acts of the European Union and the European Atomic Community, insofar as they do not make conclusive provisions for member states regarding radiological dangers.
(2) The reference values set out in this law and in the ordinances issued on the basis of this law for the protection of the population and the emergency services during emergencies should be kept as low as possible.
(3) The exposure of the population and the emergency services as well as the contamination of the environment during emergencies must be kept as low as possible through appropriate measures, taking into account the state of science and all circumstances of the respective emergency, even below the reference values. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 93 Referenzwerte für den Schutz der Bevölkerung; Verordnungsermächtigungen
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:30f54a9aa38dc2a.text:
### § 93 Referenzwerte für den Schutz der Bevölkerung; Verordnungsermächtigungen
(1) For the protection of the population, a reference value of 100 millisieverts applies when planning protection measures and making decisions on their implementation in an emergency, representing the effective dose that affected persons would receive within one year through all exposure pathways if the planned protection measures were implemented. The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to stipulate procedures and assumptions for estimating the extent to which this reference value is undercut, maintained, or exceeded.
(2) The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to stipulate supplementary appropriate reference values for organ equivalent doses for possible emergencies or for an already occurred emergency. This applies particularly to facilitate cooperation with other member states of the European Union and the European Atomic Community or third countries in the protection of the population.
(3) The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to stipulate a lower reference value for the effective dose, relative to a year or a single exposure, for an already occurred emergency. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 94 Dosiswerte und Kontaminationswerte für den Schutz der Bevölkerung; Verordnungsermächtigungen
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:03c50a4e3f50364.text:
### § 94 Dosiswerte und Kontaminationswerte für den Schutz der Bevölkerung; Verordnungsermächtigungen
(1) The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety shall stipulate, by ordinance with the consent of the Bundesrat, dose values that serve as a radiological criterion for the appropriateness of the following protection measures:
1. Recommendation to stay in buildings.
2. Distribution of iodine tablets or recommendation for the ingestion of iodine tablets.
3. Evacuation.
These values relate to the dose that affected persons would receive in a specific period after the onset of the emergency without protective measures.
(2) The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety is authorized by ordinance, with the consent of the Bundesrat, to stipulate limit values for emergency-related contaminations or dose rates for possible emergencies, for an already occurred emergency, and for an exposure situation remaining after an emergency:
1. For individual persons of the population.
2. For drinking water.
3. For food, feed, necessities, cosmetic products, and products within the meaning of § 2 Number 1 of the Tobacco Products Act.
4. For medicinal products and their starting materials, as well as for medical devices.
5. For other products, objects, and materials.
6. For vehicles, goods, or baggage.
7. For contaminated areas, especially for contaminated properties and waters, whose exceeding is to be assumed to constitute a danger for individual persons of the population due to ionizing radiation. These limit values serve the implementation of optimized protection strategies according to § 98 Paragraph 3 Sentence 1 Number 1.
(3) The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety is authorized by ordinance without the consent of the Bundesrat to repeal, declare temporarily inapplicable, or adapt the wording of the ordinances according to Paragraph 2, insofar as they have become indefinitely or temporarily inapplicable by corresponding legal acts of the European Atomic Community or the European Union.
(4) In the ordinances according to Paragraphs 1 and 2, procedures and assumptions for the measurement, calculation, or estimation of dose values, contamination values, or dose rate values may also be stipulated, or prerequisites may be stipulated under which these values apply.
(5) The ordinances according to Paragraph 2 are concluded in agreement with the Federal Ministry of Health, the Federal Ministry of Food and Agriculture, the Federal Ministry of Economics and Energy, the Federal Ministry of Labor and Social Affairs, the Federal Ministry of Transport and Digital Infrastructure, the Federal Ministry of the Interior, for Building and Home, and the Federal Ministry of Finance. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 95 Bewirtschaftung von Abfällen, die infolge eines Notfalls kontaminiert sein können, Errichtung und Betrieb von Anlagen; Verordnungsermächtigungen
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:6ea5c2d12acc3fd.text:
### § 95 Bewirtschaftung von Abfällen, die infolge eines Notfalls kontaminiert sein können, Errichtung und Betrieb von Anlagen; Verordnungsermächtigungen
(1) The Federal Government shall stipulate, by ordinance with the consent of the Bundesrat, contamination values for waste and other objects or materials that are contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency, for possible emergencies, for an already occurred emergency, and for an exposure situation remaining after an emergency. If these contamination values are undercut, it is assumed that the necessary protection of man and environment against the harmful effects of ionizing radiation is ensured when managing this waste, as well as when establishing, operating, or using the facilities listed below, in accordance with the Circular Economy Act and other federal laws applicable to waste and to the facilities based on these laws and the ordinances based on these laws without additional special protective measures:
1. Facilities where this waste is disposed of.
2. Wastewater facilities that receive wastewater that is contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency.
3. Facilities where this waste or these other objects or materials are stored, used, or treated, or may be stored, used, or treated, especially as fuel, raw material, material, precursor product, lubricating, dissolving, or other auxiliary agent.
(2) To ensure the protection of man and environment against the harmful effects of ionizing radiation, the Federal Government shall regulate by ordinance with the consent of the Bundesrat for the prevention, recovery, disposal, or other management of waste that is contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency, the additional requirements and exceptions to the following legal provisions or allow the issuance of exceptions to these legal provisions by the competent authorities:
1. to the Circular Economy Act and other federal laws applicable to waste and to the ordinances based on these laws, and
2. to federal laws governing the establishment and operation of the facilities mentioned in Paragraph 1 Sentence 2, and to the ordinances based on these laws.
Exceptions may only be regulated, permitted, or granted insofar as dangers to human health are not expected thereby and European Union or European Atomic Community acts do not contradict this. In such exceptions, significant disadvantages for the general public or the neighborhood must be avoided or reduced, insofar as this is possible and appropriate considering the radiological situation and the other considerable circumstances of the respective emergency. For the exceptions and the supplementary regulations, requirements for prevention against harmful environmental impacts and other dangers, as well as against significant disadvantages and significant nuisances, must be considered, particularly by taking measures corresponding to the state of the art.
(3) The regulations according to Paragraph 2 relate in particular to:
1. The priority of measures for waste prevention and waste management.
2. Requirements for the harmlessness of recovery.
3. The order and execution of waste disposal.
4. Requirements for the establishment and operation of landfills and their approval, including the approval procedure.
5. Requirements for the monitoring of waste management.
6. Requirements for collectors, transporters, dealers, and brokers of waste, and their respective approval including the approval procedure.
7. Requirements for the establishment, nature, operation, and essential change of the facilities mentioned in Paragraph 1 Sentence 2, the approval of these facilities including the approval procedure, as well as the condition of the facility and the facility site after cessation of operation.
8. Requirements for the use of the wastewater facilities mentioned in Paragraph 1 Sentence 2 Number 2.
9. Requirements for the use of waters, especially for the introduction and discharge of substances into a body of water; the requirements can also be stipulated for the location of the wastewater incident or before its mixing.
10. Requirements for fulfilling the wastewater disposal obligation.
11. Requirements for monitoring water properties.
12. Measurement methods and procedures, particularly in the context of wastewater disposal and monitoring of water properties.
13. Duties of the operators of the facilities mentioned in Paragraph 1 Sentence 2.
14. The prerequisites under which the competent authorities can grant exceptions by an ordinance according to Paragraph 2.
15. The requirements to be fulfilled for the obligations arising from Paragraph 2 Sentence 2 and 3.
(4) The states shall stipulate which legal persons are obliged as public law waste disposal operators in the sense of § 17 of the Circular Economy Act to dispose of such waste from private households and from other sources that cannot be treated, stored, or disposed of in the facilities intended for the disposal of other waste, due to their emergency-related contamination.
(5) For ordinances according to Paragraphs 1 to 3, § 94 Paragraph 3 and 4 apply accordingly. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:26a5484bb953c16.text:
### § 95a Auskunftsverlangen, Betretensrechte, Mitwirkungs- und Duldungspflichten
(1) Information regarding waste and other objects or materials that are contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency, regarding the establishment, operation, and use of the facilities mentioned in § 95 Paragraph 1 Sentence 2, regarding properties where such waste, other objects or materials, or such facilities may be located, as well as other objects or materials subject to supervision according to § 178 Sentence 2, must be provided to the employees and agents of the competent authority responsible for supervision according to § 178 Sentence 2 upon request:
1. Generators and owners of waste or other objects or materials that are contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency.
2. Those obliged to dispose of waste that is contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency.
3. Owners and operators, and former operators of:
    a) companies that dispose of such waste.
    b) the facilities mentioned in § 95 Paragraph 1 Sentence 2, even if these facilities are decommissioned.
4. Owners and owners of properties where the facilities mentioned in § 95 Paragraph 1 Sentence 2 are operated or were operated, as well as
5. Collectors, transporters, dealers, and brokers of waste that is contaminated or may be contaminated by an emergency.
(2) The persons obliged to provide information according to Paragraph 1 must allow the employees and agents of the competent authority responsible for supervision according to § 178 Sentence 2 to inspect the properties and business and operational premises during usual business hours, and to conduct technical investigations and inspections, including the determination of emissions and immissions, to check compliance with their obligations under § 95, the ordinances under § 95, or the interim ordinances under § 96. The persons obliged to provide information according to Paragraph 1 are furthermore obliged to allow access to the business and operational properties and premises outside of usual business hours, and access to residential premises, if this is necessary to prevent urgent dangers to public safety or order. The constitutional right to the inviolability of the home (Article 13 Paragraph 1 of the Basic Law) is restricted in this respect.
(3) Operators of the facilities mentioned in § 95 Paragraph 1 Sentence 2 must make these facilities accessible to the employees or agents of the competent authority, and must provide the necessary workforce, tools, aids, especially fuels and propulsion units, and documents for the supervision, and allow the state authority to inspect the condition and operation of the facility at its own expense upon order of the competent authority.
(4) The official powers according to Paragraphs 1 to 3 also extend to checking whether certain substances or objects:
1. are not or no longer to be regarded as waste, or
2. are to be regarded as waste, other objects or materials for which the contamination value stipulated in an ordinance according to § 95 Paragraph 1 is undercut.
(5) For the persons obliged to provide information according to this provision, § 55 of the Code of Criminal Procedure applies accordingly.
(6) The knowledge and documents obtained according to Paragraphs 1 to 3 are not subject to §§ 93, 97, 105 Paragraph 1, § 111 Paragraph 5 in conjunction with § 105 Paragraph 1, or § 116 Paragraph 1 of the Fiscal Code. This does not apply if the tax authorities require the knowledge for the execution of a tax criminal procedure and an associated tax assessment procedure, for which there is an imperative public interest in prosecution, or if it concerns intentionally false statements by the person obliged to provide information or persons acting for them. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:d476911b8489bbb.text:
### § 96 Eilverordnungen
(1) In case of urgency after an emergency, the Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety may issue regulations according to §§ 93, 94, and 95 Paragraph 1, and the Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety or the ministry responsible for waste management regulations outside the scope of the Circular Economy Act or for regulations on the establishment and operation of the facilities mentioned in § 95 Paragraph 1 Sentence 2, may issue regulations according to § 95 Paragraph 2 and 3 by ordinance without the consent of the Bundesrat and without the agreement of the participating federal ministries (Interim Ordinances), insofar as no corresponding regulations exist or the existing regulations are not adequate.
(2) Interim ordinances expire at the latest six months after their entry into force. Their duration can only be extended by an ordinance with the consent of the Bundesrat and in agreement with the participating federal ministries. Interim ordinances that change existing regulations must be repealed immediately if demanded by the Bundesrat. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:0f5f982d78f3d13.text:
### § 97 Gemeinsame Vorschriften für die Notfallpläne
(1) The Federation and the states shall draw up emergency plans according to §§ 98, 99, 100, and 101. These emergency plans must present the planned appropriate reactions to possible emergencies based on specific reference scenarios. The emergency reactions to be presented include:
1. Protection measures comprising:
    a) Measures to prevent or reduce exposure and contamination of man or environment, and
    b) Measures for medical treatment or prevention after an exposure.
2. Other measures to be taken by the participating authorities and other organizations in an emergency to prevent or keep the adverse effects of the emergency on human health or the environment as low as possible, particularly measures for the examination, preparation, execution, monitoring, amendment, or repeal of protective measures, as well as cooperation and coordination in emergencies.
(2) The emergency plans shall enable the participating authorities and organizations in the emergency to make coordinated decisions immediately and to carry out the appropriate measures in a timely manner.
(3) The authorities responsible for drafting the emergency plans:
1. shall coordinate their emergency plans insofar as this is necessary for the preparation of a coordinated emergency response, and
2. shall endeavor, within their respective responsibilities, to coordinate their emergency plans with other member states of the European Union and the European Atomic Community, as well as with third states according to the principles of reciprocity and equivalence.
(4) A select circle of representatives from science, the affected economy, environmental associations, municipalities and municipal associations, organizations involved in emergency preparedness and response, as well as other interested parties and the highest state authorities responsible for the respective area, shall be invited to the drafts of the emergency plans of the Federation, the ordinances according to §§ 93 to 95 and 117 Paragraph 1, and the drafts of essential amendments to these emergency plans and ordinances. Sentence 1 does not apply to the enactment of interim ordinances according to §§ 93 to 95 and 117 Paragraph 2, nor to the enactment, amendments, and supplements of ordinances and emergency plans for an emergency according to §§ 94 and 111. For the drafts of the general and special emergency plans of the states, a select circle of interested parties shall be invited by the state. The states may limit the invitation to relevant state or area-specific concretizations or supplements of the optimized protection strategies and measures provided for in the federal emergency plans. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:dbadd9986f52bf5.text:
### § 98 Allgemeiner Notfallplan des Bundes
(1) The Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, and Nuclear Safety shall evaluate possible emergency exposure situations. The Federal Government shall issue a general emergency plan of the Federation based on its proposal. The general emergency plan of the Federation shall be adopted as a general administrative regulation with the consent of the Bundesrat.
(2) The general emergency plan of the Federation must include:
1. Reference scenarios that serve as the basis for the planning of emergency responses for the Federation and the states, and
2. The following general plans for possible emergencies within or outside the scope of this law:
    a) the plans of the Federation,
    b) the plans of the European Union and the European Atomic Community, their member states, and third states, and
    c) the plans of international organizations and the plans within the framework of international treaties.
(3) The general emergency plan of the Federation includes in particular:
1. Strategies optimally adapted to the respective reference scenario for the protection of the population and the emergency services, which also consider particularly vulnerable persons (optimized protection strategies), and
2. The further elements listed in Appendix 5.
The general emergency plan of the Federation may also contain indications of the emergency plans of the states, municipalities, and municipal associations, as well as of other organizations involved in emergency preparedness and response, or summarize these emergency plans. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:56ec59290249c72.text:
### § 99 Besondere Notfallpläne des Bundes
(1) Upon proposal from the federal ministries responsible for the respective subject areas, the Federal Government supplements and concretizes the general emergency plan of the Federation with special emergency plans of the Federation. The special emergency plans of the Federation shall be adopted as general administrative regulations with the consent of the Bundesrat.
(2) The special emergency plans of the Federation must present the plans in particular for the following areas of application:
1. For disaster protection, general danger control and assistance, as well as for medical treatment and prevention after an exposure of the population and the emergency services,
2. For drinking water extraction and supply,
3. For the production of plant and animal products, for food, feed, necessities, cosmetic products, and products within the meaning of § 2 Number 1 of the Tobacco Products Act,
4. For medicinal products and their starting materials, as well as for medical devices,
5. For other products, objects, and materials,
6. For the transport of goods,
7. For the cross-border transport of persons, vehicles, goods, and baggage,
8. For contaminated areas, especially for contaminated properties and waters,
9. For the disposal of waste and the disposal of wastewater, as well as for the establishment and operation of the facilities mentioned in § 95 Paragraph 1 Sentence 2.
(3) The special emergency plans include in particular the elements listed in Appendix 6. § 98 Paragraph 3 Sentence 2 applies accordingly. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 100 Allgemeine und besondere Notfallpläne der Länder
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:9175d9074aa573b.text:
### § 100 Allgemeine und besondere Notfallpläne der Länder
The states shall draw up general and special emergency plans. These emergency plans of the states supplement and concretize the general emergency plan of the Federation and the special emergency plans of the Federation, insofar as the states are responsible for the planning or execution of protective measures. [StrlSchG.md]

heading_path: § 101 Externe Notfallpläne für ortsfeste Anlagen oder Einrichtungen mit besonderem Gefahrenpotential
wiki:summary-strlschg.md:084816ded447a27.text:
### § 101 Externe Notfallpläne für ortsfeste Anlagen oder Einrichtungen mit besonderem Gefahrenpotential
(1) The authorities responsible for disaster protection or public safety shall draw up special protection plans (external emergency plans) according to their state law provisions for the surroundings of nuclear facilities, facilities according to § 9a Paragraph 3 Sentence 1 second sentence of the Atomic Energy Act, facilities for the generation of ionizing radiation, or facilities in the sense of § 5 Paragraph 12 of this law, insofar as emergencies in the facility or facility can lead to severe health impairments for a non-negligible number of persons in the vicinity of the facility or facility.
(2) The external emergency plans supplement and concretize the plans contained in the general and special emergency plans of the Federation and the states. They take into account the local conditions as well as the procedures and precautions of the radiation protection officer for the internal emergency protection of the facility. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:535cb3b085da249.text:
### § 102 Notfallübungen
(1) The authorities and organizations involved in the emergency response according to the emergency plans of the Federation and the states, as well as those responsible for the training and further education of the emergency services according to § 115 Paragraph 1, shall regularly conduct emergency exercises.
(2) The emergency exercises must be appropriately differentiated according to the type of exercise, scope, emergency scenarios, and participants. In particular, the organizational precautions for the emergency response and the exchange of information and cooperation of the authorities, organizations, and radiation protection officers involved in the emergency response must be tested and practiced regarding:
a) situation assessment and situation evaluation,
b) the coordination of the decisions of the competent authorities, and
c) the execution of appropriate protective measures. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:451f335ff44cb32.text:
### § 103 Überprüfung und Änderung der Notfallpläne
(1) The emergency plans of the Federation and the states shall be regularly reviewed and, if necessary, amended, taking into account the experiences from emergency exercises, findings from emergencies domestically or abroad, and changes in the state of science and law.
(2) The information supplementing the emergency plans, such as the contact details of the competent authorities and participating organizations or the directories of applicable legal provisions, shall be updated and regularly reviewed upon changes. The deadlines for the reviews must be stipulated in the emergency plans. [StrlSchG.md]

wiki:summary-strlschg.md:4554bbc67d25de9.text:
### § 104 Beschaffung von Schutzwirkstoffen
